title: Seviyan Vermicelli

seo.title: Seviyan Vermicelli Recipe: Easy Pakistani Sweet Dish
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Seviyan Vermicelli with our easy Pakistani recipe. This sweet vermicelli dessert is perfect for any occasion. Get the full recipe now!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of traditional Pakistani Seviyan Vermicelli, a sweet vermicelli dessert.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this delightful Seviyan Vermicelli, a classic Pakistani sweet dish perfect for celebrations or a comforting treat.

description: Seviyan Vermicelli is a beloved Pakistani sweet dish, often served on festive occasions like Eid or as a comforting dessert. This delightful treat combines roasted vermicelli with milk, sugar, and aromatic spices, creating a rich and creamy texture. Garnished with nuts and dried fruits, it offers a perfect balance of sweetness and warmth, making it a truly irresistible culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: lightly crushed, or 1/2 tsp cardamom powder
optional, soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk

instructions.title: Roast the Vermicelli
instructions.text: Heat ghee or butter in a heavy-bottomed pan or pot over medium-low heat. Add the broken vermicelli pieces and roast them, stirring continuously, until they turn a light golden brown and become fragrant. This usually takes about 5-7 minutes. Be careful not to burn them. Transfer the roasted vermicelli to a plate and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Milk Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the full-fat milk and bring it to a gentle boil over medium heat. Once it starts to boil, reduce the heat to low.

instructions.title: Add Sugar and Aromatics
instructions.text: To the simmering milk, add the granulated sugar and crushed green cardamom pods (or cardamom powder). Stir well until the sugar completely dissolves. If using saffron, add the saffron strands soaked in warm milk at this stage.

instructions.title: Combine Vermicelli and Milk
instructions.text: Carefully add the roasted vermicelli back into the simmering milk mixture. Stir gently to ensure all the vermicelli is submerged in the milk. Add the raisins now if using.

instructions.title: Simmer and Absorb
instructions.text: Cover the pan with a lid and let it simmer on very low heat for about 10-15 minutes, or until the vermicelli becomes tender and has absorbed most of the milk. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. The mixture should be creamy but not completely dry.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the seviyan is cooked, remove it from the heat. Stir in half of the slivered almonds and pistachios. Transfer the Seviyan Vermicelli to serving bowls. Garnish with the remaining slivered almonds, pistachios, and desiccated coconut (if using). Serve hot or warm as a delightful Pakistani sweet treat.

tips: Always roast the vermicelli on low heat to ensure even browning and prevent burning, which can give a bitter taste.
For extra richness, you can use condensed milk instead of regular milk and sugar, adjusting the quantity based on desired sweetness.
Seviyan Vermicelli can be served hot, warm, or even chilled. For a thicker consistency, let it sit for a while before serving as it will continue to absorb liquid.
article: Seviyan Vermicelli is a beloved Pakistani Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
